meta_title: For Students: testing automation to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to testing automation and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Testing Automation to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no project ideas' block by building automated testing tools. A beginner-friendly case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for your portfolio.
content.answer_block: Stop searching for project ideas and start building utility tools. By automating the testing of common web interactions, you solve a real developer pain point. This beginner-friendly project teaches you testing frameworks, CLI interaction, and AI-assisted debugging—the exact skills recruiters look for in junior engineering candidates today.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the developer's greatest enemy. You’ve mastered the syntax, watched the tutorials, and memorized the docs, but when you open your code editor, the cursor just blinks. The 'no project idea' paralysis is where most aspiring engineers quit, stuck in a loop of endless consumption without the friction of actual creation.

But what if you stopped hunting for a 'big idea' and started solving a developer workflow problem? Testing automation isn't just a chore; it is the backbone of professional software engineering. By building an automated test suite for a mock application, you transition from a student writing code to an engineer building robust, production-ready systems.

steps.title: Define the Automation Scope
steps.description: Identify a simple web form to test. We will target a login page to ensure the 'submit' button triggers the correct validation error.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a boilerplate Playwright test file structure that tests a login form with empty credentials, asserting that an error message appears.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create --description 'Initialize a new Playwright project with a login test suite'

steps.title: Configure the Test Environment
steps.description: Set up your environment variables and dependencies so your tests run in a headless browser.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a configuration script that handles environment variables for local testing and sets the base URL for the login page.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'install playwright and configure base URL in playwright.config.ts'

steps.title: Implement the Core Test Logic
steps.description: Write the actual test code to simulate user input and capture the response.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a test function that navigates to the login page, fills the username and password fields, clicks submit, and validates the error text.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'add a test case for invalid login credentials in login.spec.ts'

steps.title: Add Automation Reporting
steps.description: Enhance your project by adding a CLI report generator that logs test results to the console.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a custom reporter function that outputs a formatted summary of passed or failed tests to the terminal.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add a custom reporter to the playwright config to output results to the console'

steps.title: Stress Test and Refine
steps.description: Run your suite against a local server to ensure stability and fix any flaky test behaviors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current test suite and suggest three ways to make the selectors more robust against UI changes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'execute tests in parallel and provide a summary of execution time'

pro_tips: Always test for the 'negative' path first; developers often over-test success, but bugs live in the edge cases.
Use data-testid attributes instead of CSS classes for selectors to prevent your tests from breaking during UI redesigns.
Automate your test execution on every Git commit using a simple shell script to learn the basics of CI/CD.
Keep your tests isolated; if one test relies on the state of a previous one, you've created a maintenance nightmare.
Document your test strategy in a README file; recruiters value documentation as much as the code itself.
content.next_steps: After completing your test suite, push the project to GitHub with a detailed README, then try adding a visual regression test to your suite to expand your automation portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You now have a functional, professional-grade testing suite that proves you understand quality assurance—a rare skill for a beginner. Stop waiting for the perfect idea and start shipping the work that gets you hired. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
